author: bharath.vedartham
content:
yeah i can ask. But I feel we won't get useful feedback now from them since I am not aware of any builder so far who has started adapting their infra with ePBS. 
Speaking from building buildoor, i didn't have to use it to get things to work on devnets.

author: bharath.vedartham
content: To understand the pool better, when a beacon node receives a proposer preferences they would cache it in their proposer preferences cache right? is that the same as the pool or would that be different. Would the cache only contain preferences for the proposers we are tracking and the pool contain all the preferences we have seen?

content: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/606 anyone have thoughts on allowing extra fields in peers data?

author: nflaig
content: a node needs to track all proposer preferences since those are required to validate bids, so serving the data should be easy

author: nflaig
content: general question I have, do we need a v2? it only adds new fields + is json only, we might be fine with keeping v1 and adding new fields

author: nflaig
content: v2 is the clean way to go about it but keeping v1 might be practical in this case, curious if anyone, maybe <@84562395988508672> has an opinion, I am not aware of many consumers of this, https://github.com/ethpandaops/forky should be one

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
I am working on implementing the builder-api in buildoor and prototyping it in prysm. I want to raise a point:
When we are proposing a block, the validator client calls the beacon node to get the beacon block, the validator client signs the block, and then sends the signed block back to the beacon node to broadcast it. 
When the signed block is sent to the beacon node, this is when beacon nodes check whether to call the `getPayload` API to the builder. This is called if the block is blinded which is easy to check pre gloas. 
Post gloas, this will get hard cause you cannot distinguish if a bid is from the builder api or from the p2p.

author: bharath.vedartham
content: In my prysm prototype, b/w both the calls I maintain a cache b/w block root -> bid type where I mark whether the bid is from the builder-api or not. But I am not sure if this is the correct solution

author: nflaig
content: for builder api purposes, don't you even wanna "remember" from which builder (based on url/or index) you got the bid?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yeah exactly, cause today we can call the `getPayload` API to all relays and its fine but with ePBS, we need to send the beacon block back only to the winning builder

author: bharath.vedartham
content: because only they can reveal the payload

author: nflaig
content: let's get rid of this api altogether maybe? technically it shouldn't be needed, they can observe that block via p2p

author: bharath.vedartham
content: here we will rely on proposers doing all work to broadcast the block, maybe thats fine? I kept the endpoint because I thought even the builder can help in block broadcast in this case

author: bharath.vedartham
content: today the relays also help in broadcasting the block as we assume that these guys have really good n/w infra. But today we also have the full payload in the block, maybe this api doesn't matter hmm
